other_observers: No other birders were present during my visit.; Seen previously by many others since the bird was originally reported to the CoBirds mailing list on 19 Nov by Joey Kellner.
specific_location: Seen from the handicapped fisherman's platform near the marina in the middle of the reservoir. The bird circled the reservoir multiple times, occasionally flying out over the dam as well as disappearing over the south-western part of the reservoir.
description: Dark bird with a paler brown rump, and white bases to the primaries and greater primary coverts giving the distinctive "double flash" of pale on the underwing. Bill was stout with a black tip, feet were black, legs pale gray. Brown underparts were finely barred, including belly, undertail/vent and wing linings. Slightly smaller than a Herring Gull but had a proportionally heavier body and proportionally smaller head. Tail slightly wedge shaped due to protruding central tail feathers.Behaviors: Seen circling the reservoir, twice giving chase to Ring-billed Gulls. It never got a meal from either of them (nor did it make a meal of either of them) as far as I could tell.Call: nonePlumage: light intermediate immature
